text: Barack Obama: John McCain opposed bankruptcy protections for families "who were only in bankruptcy because of medical expenses they couldn't pay."
S.E. Cupp: "There are actually only 30 countries that practice birthright citizenship."
George Turner: "If you go strictly by the numbers, crime is down across the board. Last year we had a 10 percent decrease in the most serious crimes."
Mandela Barnes: "Since 1978, CEO compensation rose over 1,000% and only 11.9% for average workers."
Pete Buttigieg: "There is racism physically built into some of our highways."
Rudy Giuliani: Financial conflict-of-interest "laws don't apply to the president, right? So, the president doesn't have to have a blind trust."
Tennessee Democratic Party: Says a federal judge "sanctions Tennessee ‘birther’ lawyer … for bringing a frivolous lawsuit."
John Boehner: "PWNED: House GOP Dominates Twitter, YouTube, Social Media in Congress."
Mike Haridopolos: "Whether you like to admit it or not, half our general revenue goes to education."
Martin Kiar: "You can go to Georgia and make about $6,000 more a year as a teacher."
John McCain: "Sen. Obama has declared, and repeatedly reaffirmed his intention to meet the president of Iran without any preconditions."
Ron Amstutz: The budget currently being debated "significantly decreases the use of one-time resources."
David Dooley: "We have to recognize that our salaries for faculty are the lowest in New England with the exception of the University of Maine."
Joe Manchin: "This year West Virginia produced more AmeriCorps members than any other state."
Jeff Kottkamp: "I've taken on the federal government and won."
Tom Niehaus: "Unlike the gridlock in Washington, more than 80 percent of the bills we passed during this General Assembly had bipartisan support."
Brendan Doherty: "David Cicilline was the state representative who opposed tough mandatory sentences for those convicted of domestic violence  and child abuse."
Jay Nixon: Says that "unlike Texas, Missouri has a perfect AAA credit rating."
Bing West: Seventy-five percent of the young adults in this country are not mentally or physically fit to serve.
David Dewhurst: Says Barack Obama has played over 90 rounds of golf as president.
Tammy Baldwin: 29 states still don’t have "comprehensive protections" for people in the LGBTQ community.
National Right to Life Committee: Barack Obama voted against "born alive" legislation in Illinois that was "virtually identical to the federal law."
Barack Obama: When Mitt Romney was governor of Massachusetts, he "stood in front of a coal plant and pointed at it and said, ‘This plant kills.’"
Media Matters: Says Fox News gave Republican senators twice the air time as Democrats during a hearing about the Islamic State.
Barack Obama: John McCain wants to "give oil companies another $4-billion" in tax breaks.
Barack Obama: "More than half of all black children live in single-parent households, a number that has doubled — doubled — since we were children."
Kirk Cox: On Virginia’s new marijuana law: "You can have up to one pound with only a $25 fine."
WrongWayRubio.com: Says that U.S. Sen. Marco Rubio says troops are leaving Iraq "too fast."
Jim Cooper: "Congress as a whole is less popular than it’s been since polling was invented."
David Jolly: Says he "was the only Republican" to vote against creating a House panel to investigate Planned Parenthood.
JoEllen Smith: Cobb is one of the largest school systems (in the nation) that is debt free.
American Petroleum Institute: Says "Bill Clinton and George Bush both say build the Keystone XL."
Charles Schumer: "President Obama became the first president since Carter to leave the White House with a smaller federal prison population than when he took office."
Debbie Wasserman Schultz: Says Jeb Bush "flip-flop-flip(ped) on immigration."
Mary Nolan: Says "as Co-Chair of the Joint Ways & Means Committee, I secured the key piece of funding for" the Portland-Milwaukie Light Rail project bridge."
Eric Cantor: "You’ve got each day 10,000 new seniors, baby boomers, becoming eligible for the entitlement programs."
Sam Adams: Four out of 10 homicides are committed by gun in this city.
Jennifer Boysko: "The United States is the only industrialized, modernized country that does not already have a paid family medical leave program in place."
Jay Rockefeller: Health care premiums for consumers have doubled since 2001.
Major Garrett: "It's been nearly three weeks since the president has commented" on questions about possible hidden wait lists at veterans hospitals.
Hillary Clinton: Tax rates were significantly higher "in the '40s, the '50s, and the '60s."
Joe Biden: "I’ve released 22 years of my tax returns. You can go online and look. (Donald Trump) hasn’t released one."
Michael McCaul: "I stopped requesting earmarks in 2008."
David Brooks: Preventive care does not save the government money.
Mike Scott: "Trey Radel does not even qualify to drive a Lee County school bus at this point, yet he occupies a seat in Congress."
Richard Blumenthal: "There's never been a (U.S. Supreme Court) vacancy in the last, I think, 30 years, lasting longer than 237 days."
One Wisconsin Now: It’s legal to "sign a recall petition (against Gov. Scott Walker) even if you have already signed another recall petition," but only one signature counts
Frederica Wilson: "We closed that plant down. It was a $27 million-dollar plant, operating full steam, and we closed it down."

Alex Sink: House Republicans who complained they didn't have enough time to consider a constitutional ban against oil drilling "shoved through a proposal in just a few days to open state waters to oil drilling."
Hillary Clinton: "Again today (Ahmadinejad) made light of 9/11, and said that he's not even sure it happened and that people actually died."
National Republican Congressional Committee: "Even if you took literally every single ounce of platinum in the world, it still wouldn’t add up to enough needed to pay down our nation’s debt."

David Cicilline: "If you are a federally licensed gun dealer and your license is revoked because you've engaged in misconduct ... your entire inventory under prevailing law is deemed your personal collection. You then can sell it free from any background checks."
Ann Wagner: "Since passage of the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, Walmart 'increased their starting wage to $11/hr & has given about $400 million in bonuses.'"
Greg Abbott: Says Texas ranks among the nation’s top five states for its high school graduation rate.
Jim Sensenbrenner: "We have the highest corporate tax rate in the world. It’s 35 percent."
Mike Romano: The poverty rate in West Virginia "was 19.1 percent, the fourth-highest in the country."
Tim Kaine: "The national economy was strong in its largest expansion of private sector jobs before President Trump came into office."
Rick Scott: "At nearly 19 million people, the population of Florida is larger than all the earlier primary and caucus states combined."

Rob Turner: The property appraiser's budget "is $1 million less today than when I took office 16 years ago."
Chris Koster: "Closing the tobacco loophole would save Missouri taxpayers $50,000,000"
Eric Cantor: Wayne Powell has a "stated position of having no objection to taking `In God We Trust" off of U.S. currency."
Virginia Senate Democratic Caucus: The House of Delegates budget bill "cuts $50 million from education."

John Kasich: "I think [a little] less than half of the people in our prisons are in there for less than a year.  ... We have people who are check kiters and don't pay child support and we are locking them up in the state pen."
Donald Trump: The Dow Jones Industrial Average "jumped 1000 points in last 5 weeks, Record fastest 1000 point move in history."
Samuel Sinyangwe: "America has more governors who’ve worn blackface than black governors."
Tim Ryan: The average person "will pay $6,000 more a year" under Rep. Paul Ryan's Medicare proposal.
Rick Scott: Florida "unemployment has dropped more than 2 percentage points, down from 12 percent to 9.9 percent, the second-largest drop in the nation."
Tim Kaine: "Virginia women are paid 80 cents for every dollar paid to Virginia men."
Bill Clinton: The Obama administration is allowing state waivers from welfare work requirements "only if they had a credible plan to increase employment by 20 percent."
Mike Dudgeon: Thirty-eight states -- Georgia not included -- have appointed, rather than elected, state school superintendents.
Jeff Sessions: "One out of every 30 people in the Greater Boston area is an illegal alien."
Sherrod Brown: Says that Wall Street hedge fund managers "pay a lower tax rate than does a sheet metal worker in Parma or a school teacher in Cleveland."
David Plouffe: "Gov. Romney was very clear, he would veto the DREAM Act."

Ed FitzGerald: Cuyahoga County "passed a program to guarantee a college savings account for every single child who enters kindergarten in this county -- the first county in the United States to do so."
Lincoln Chafee: The state budget proposal has been submitted "on time and [it's] the earliest that a governor has done so in over two decades."
Leonidas Raptakis: Rhode Island is "almost dead last" among Northeastern states in the length of time first-degree murderers must spend in prison before they’re eligible for parole.
Bill Haslam: Says Tennessee’s higher education funding model "is 100 percent outcomes-based" and "we are already seeing this model changing the way our postsecondary institutions do business."

Barack Obama: Some Republicans who voted against the stimulus "have been all too happy to claim credit for Recovery Act projects and the jobs those projects have produced. They come to the ribbon cuttings."
Kernan "Kerry" King: In Rhode Island "there are 150 different pension plans for public workers."
Kelly Ayotte: Says Maggie Hassan was "out of state on 30 days over the last three months."
Kent Conrad: "The American people say, don't touch Social Security, don't touch Medicare, don't cut defense. That's 84 percent of the federal budget."
Hillary Clinton: Says "Donald Trump says climate change is a hoax invented by the Chinese."
Stephen Carter: "More than 70 percent of American adults have committed a crime that could lead to imprisonment."
Roberta Lange: Says Donald Trump "literally has no plans to make higher education more affordable."
Chris Christie: "In the entire public school system in Camden last year, only three students graduated college ready."
Jay Wiley: Austin has "over 1,000 city employees that make six-figure salaries."
Patrick Murphy: Says Marco Rubio "has a 98 percent voting record with the Koch brothers."
Chris Van Hollen: "The Republicans have demanded deep cuts in education. They've demanded cuts in cancer research and other research to find cures and treatments to diseases."
John Kitzhaber: "Creating 25 jobs in Umatilla County has the same impact as 500 jobs in the metro region."
Kendrick Meek: "When I become the nominee, the Democratic nominee, I will be the only pro-choice candidate on the ballot."
Chris Christie: "Given how expansive our program already was," expanding Medicaid in New Jersey due to Obamacare "was a relatively small expansion."
Joe Biden: "All the billionaires in America, their net worth combined … has increased by $800 billion" during the pandemic.
Van Jones: African-Americans don't use drugs at a higher level than whites but "wind up going to prison six times more."
Wayne LaPierre: "By the time I finish this speech, two Americans will be slain, six women will be raped, 27 of us will be robbed, and 50 more will be beaten."
Newt Gingrich: The EPA was asked about an environmental citation for the city landfill in Nashua, N.H. but "didn’t know" why it was cited.
Ted Cruz: Says Beto O’Rourke is "open to abolishing ICE."
John Cornyn: According to the U.S. Census Bureau, 71.2 percent of Hispanic registered voters in Texas and over 86 percent of African American registered voters participated in the 2012 elections.
Barry DuVal: "We're the only major oil-producing nation in the world with a self-imposed ban on exporting our crude oil to other nations."
U.S. Chamber of Commerce: "A Florida judge ruled parts of Obamacare unconstitutional."
Gwen Graham: "So far this year, more students have been killed in schools than soldiers in combat zones."
Robin Vos: Says 1,000 people failed gun background checks in Wisconsin in 2018, but "almost none of them were prosecuted for trying to do a background check and illegally buy a weapon."
Mike Crute: Says Bernie Sanders "is the most popular politician in America today."
Hillary Clinton: Says Dontre Hamilton, who was killed during a confrontation with a Milwaukee police officer, was "unarmed."
Catherine Cortez Masto: Adam Laxalt "wants to restrict" and "worked to limit access to birth control."
Bernie Sanders: "Over half of the black workers in this country earn less" than $15 an hour.
Beto O'Rourke: Texas is "50th in the nation in mental health care access."
Tim Finchem: "Austin’s the fastest-growing city in the country the last five years."
Donald Trump: "Ford is moving all of their small-car production to Mexico."
David Miliband: "The geography of poverty has changed fundamentally in the last 15 years," with more of it concentrated in fragile and conflict states.
Jeb Bush: Says that during his eight years as Florida governor, "we created 1.3 million net new jobs -- more jobs created than Texas."
Michele Bachmann: In 1993, Newt Gingrich "first advocated for the individual mandate in health care. And as recently as May of this year, he was still advocating" for it.

Barack Obama: "America is No. 1 in wind power."
Newt Gingrich: In economic growth, "Obama's best year was slower than Bill Clinton's worst year."

Ted Cruz: "A majority of the candidates on this stage have supported amnesty ... I have never supported amnesty."

Frank Guinta: $50,000. That's what every child owes today because Washington politicians have run up a huge spending tab and left our kids the bill."
Alma Adams: "The President wants to cut 3 MILLION people from SNAP, including children, seniors and veterans."

Marco Rubio: "The child tax credit, if we don't do it, there will be no tax relief for working families. How much tax relief working families get under tax reform is entirely dependent on whether or not we put in place an increase to the child tax credit."

Jeb Bush: "When he (Donald Trump) asked Florida to have casino gambling, we said no."

Rick Scott: "We cut business taxes so today 70 percent of our businesses don't pay a business tax."

Joe Manchin: "President Trump has the strongest support percentage-wise in our state than any other state in the nation. He won West Virginia by almost 43 percentage points and he's been carrying close to 60 percent approval."
Dennis Richardson: "We are 49th in the nation for our graduation rate"
John Hickenlooper: "We got to almost universal coverage in health care in Colorado."
Richard Burr: "The pace of missile development between (Kim Jong Il and Kim Jong Un) has increased the testing about twenty-fold."

James Dobson: Says Ted Cruz’s wife Heidi "will be the very first pro-life first lady."
Michael Caputo: Says Donald Trump "spent the least amount of money of any of the competitive primary contenders that he beat so badly."

Barack Obama: "Factories are opening their doors at the fastest pace in almost two decades."

U.S. Chamber of Commerce: "Obamacare (hit) Americans with over $500 billion in new taxes."
Andy Gronik: Wisconsin is "dead-last in creating new businesses" for "three years in a row."

Mitt Romney: "Obamacare takes $500 billion out of Medicare and funds Obamacare."

Facebook posts: "It’s not that there’s a virus shield between Canada (4,500 deaths) and the U.S. (80,000 deaths). What exists is a difference in leadership."

Paul Broun: "In the last two years, I have supported and proposed $155 billion worth of targeting cuts. Dr. Gingrey has presented zero."
Rob Portman: Says the health care reform bill was paid for, in part, by taking money from students and the student loan system.

Michael Bloomberg: "I certainly supported Barack Obama and Joe Biden. ... I was there both times for them."
Hans von Spakovsky: "134,000 (criminal) aliens have been released by the (Obama) administration in just the past two years."

Chain email: "Barack Obama's nominee for 'regulatory czar' has advocated a 'Fairness Doctrine' for the Internet" that would require links to opposing opinions.
America's Health Insurance Plans: Seniors with coverage under Medicare Advantage will give up "more than their share" because of health care reform
Viral image: "Dems Pass Bill to Vaccinate Children Without Parental Knowledge, Consent."
Hillary Clinton: Says she will "work to raise the federal minimum wage back to the highest level it’s ever been — $12 an hour in today’s dollars."
Reince Priebus: Says Barack Obama has "the worst record of any president when it comes to putting America deeper in debt."
Joe Biden: Says Republicans Mitt Romney and Paul Ryan support "massive cuts in Social Security for future generations."

Doug LaMalfa: Voting-rights legislation proposed by Democrats in Congress called H.R. 1 "would force states to restore the voting rights of convicted felons—including violent felons convicted of murder or rape."

Republican National Committee: Hillary Clinton was against setting a deadline for withdrawal, but now she wants a "precipitous withdrawal" regardless of "conditions on the ground" or "consequences of a defeat for the United States," an RNC radio ad.
Gail Collins: Says Texas GOP platform calls for end to teaching "critical thinking" in public schools.
Tom Steyer: "People haven't had a raise -- 90 percent of Americans have not had a raise for 40 years."
Donald Trump: "Apple has just announced it plans to invest a total of $350 billion in America, and hire another 20,000 workers."

Bob McDonnell: In the past decade, K-12 funding has grown six times faster than the rate of enrollment.

Martin O'Malley: "Today in America, 70 percent of us are earning the same or less than we were 12 years ago, and this is the first time that that has happened this side of World War II."

Randy Forbes: "The average age a child is trafficked into the commercial sex trade industry is between 11 and 14 years old."

Ted Cruz: Says the claim that 40 percent of guns are sold without background checks traces to information gathered before background checks were even required.

Rick Perry: "The city of Houston now has more debt per capita than California."
